codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly addresses all 5 metadata getter inconsistencies with good parity to the Python source. The code is well-structured, thoroughly tested (20 new tests), and follows the project conventions. Found one non-blocking parity issue with isImageBasedFixedLayout hardcoding isScribeNotebook=false, and a pre-existing test weakness in TestIsKfxV1_Version2_False.

issues.description: isImageBasedFixedLayout hardcodes isScribeNotebook=false when calling isFixedLayout. Python's get_ordered_image_resources internally calls self.is_fixed_layout which includes the scribe_notebook check. A scribe notebook with image resources would incorrectly return false from this function. This is currently mitigated by getOrderedImageResources being a stub, so no practical impact yet. Should be fixed when A5 scope is implemented or the function should accept isScribeNotebook as a parameter.

fixCoverImageData uses exact string comparison (fmt == "jpg" || fmt == "jpeg") while Python uses fmt.lower() in ["jpg", "jpeg"]. Currently safe because getCoverImageData normalizes format to lowercase, but deviates from Python's case-insensitive comparison.

Pre-existing test TestIsKfxV1_Version2_False does not set FragmentIDsByType["$270"], so isKfxV1 returns false because no $270 fragments are found, NOT because version=2. The test passes for the wrong reason. Should be updated to include FragmentIDsByType["$270"] = []string{"gen1"} to properly test the version=2 case. This is a pre-existing issue not introduced by this commit.

reencodeJPEG uses Quality: 95 while Python uses quality='keep' which preserves original JPEG quality. Go's image/jpeg doesn't support 'keep' mode. The 95 quality is a reasonable approximation but may produce slightly different file sizes or quality than Python's output. Documented in code comments.

jpegType is ported from Python resources.py:725-747 but placed in yj_metadata_getters.go instead of a resources.go file. This violates structural parity (AGENTS.md: 'If python has a file in one place, we have a go file with the same name in the same corresponding place'). Also missing the fmt parameter from Python's jpeg_type(data, fmt='jpg') signature, though it's only called in JPEG contexts where fmt is always 'jpg'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker placed jpegType (from resources.py:725-747) in yj_metadata_getters.go instead of a dedicated resources.go file. AGENTS.md says 'If python has a file in one place, we have a go file with the same name in the same corresponding place.' There is no resources.go file in the project yet. This pattern may recur as more resources.py functions are ported.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/kfx/yj_metadata_getters.go:534 — jpegType function placed here instead of a new internal/kfx/resources.go corresponding to REFERENCE/Calibre_KFX_Input/kfxlib/resources.py

sharedStateObservations.observation: Several metadata getter functions that should accept isScribeNotebook (like isImageBasedFixedLayout) hardcode it to false. The pattern of passing boolean flags from BookMetadata-level context down to getter functions needs a documented approach — either pass as parameters or store in a context struct.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/kfx/yj_metadata_getters.go:337 — isFixedLayout(cat, false, ci) hardcodes isScribeNotebook=false in isImageBasedFixedLayout

summary: PASS with minor observations. All 5 metadata getter fixes (B5-5 through B5-9) are correctly implemented with good 1:1 logic-level parity to the Python source. The 20 new tests provide thorough coverage including edge cases like corrupt JPEG data, deep $157 nesting, placeholder filtering, and first-fragment-only checking. Key non-blocking issues: (1) isImageBasedFixedLayout hardcodes isScribeNotebook=false which will cause incorrect behavior for scribe notebooks once getOrderedImageResources is fully implemented, (2) jpegType is structurally misplaced per project conventions (should be in resources.go not yj_metadata_getters.go), (3) reencodeJPEG uses Quality:95 instead of Python's quality='keep' — a known Go limitation. The pre-existing TestIsKfxV1_Version2_False test is also weak (passes for wrong reason). All tests pass successfully.
